text.title: Agency Acquisition Prep Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed across deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Acquirer pulls LOI after diligence; reason: 'too founder-dependent'.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder is sole holder of client relationships, decisions, and key IP; dependency >60%.
antipattern.fix: Run the founder-dependency reduction track over 12-18 months: document playbooks, transfer client owners, redact founder from delivery.

antipattern.symptom: Pitch deck reads like a financial-buyer deck for a strategic-buyer meeting; meeting goes cold.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder optimised for both lanes simultaneously; metrics, narrative, and asks are diluted.
antipattern.fix: Pick the lane per cycle; tailor materials accordingly. Strategic = synergy & integration. Financial = EBITDA, retention, scale-without-founder.

antipattern.symptom: Inbound LOI received; data room takes 6 weeks to assemble; competing bidders walk.
antipattern.root-cause: Founder never maintained a rolling data room; everything is gathered reactively.
antipattern.fix: Maintain rolling weekly data room: P&L, payroll roster, client retainer status, IP inventory, contracts.

antipattern.symptom: Founder signs exclusive LOI with single bidder; valuation drops 25% during diligence with no leverage to walk.
antipattern.root-cause: Verbal commitment to one bidder eliminated competitive tension before LOI hygiene review.
antipattern.fix: Enforce LOI hygiene checklist before signing: exclusivity ≤45 days, earnout cap, IP carve-outs, non-compete radius.
